# JVD 3-Stage Data Center (EVPN-VXLAN) snippet library

## evo/bootstrap/chassis-port-config.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   Chassis port-speed configuration for QFX5220/5130/5700 spine and border leaf
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: (none)
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — port speed assignments follow Apstra intent model.
 *  - All ports explicitly set to $PORT_SPEED; Apstra requires explicit speed even
 *    when it matches the hardware default.
 *  - QFX5220 spine uses 16 ports; QFX5130 border leaf uses 32+2 ports with breakout
 *    capability on ports 8–19.
 *  - Platform mapping: QFX5220-32CD and QFX5130-32CD use et-0/0/N; QFX5700 uses
 *    et-0/0/N with dual-RE chassis redundancy block; ACX7100-48L uses et-0/0/N;
 *    PTX10001-36MR uses et-0/0/N.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/interfaces/fabric-uplink.conf  (ports configured here carry the fabric links)
 *  - evo/bootstrap/grpc-certificate.conf           (grpc-certificate)
 *
 * Variables (example values from spine1_qfx5220-32cd):
 *   $PORT_SPEED    e.g. 100g
 *   $FPC_SLOT      e.g. 0
 *   $PIC_SLOT      e.g. 0
 *   $PORT_RANGE    e.g. 0–15
 */
chassis {
    fpc $FPC_SLOT {
        pic $PIC_SLOT {
            port $PORT_NUM {
                speed $PORT_SPEED;
            }
            /* repeat for each port in range */
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/bootstrap/grpc-certificate.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   gRPC TLS certificate for Juniper Apstra controller authentication
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: (none)
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — the certificate name "aos_grpc" is the Apstra
 *    convention (AOS = Apstra Operating System, the former product name).
 *  - SECRET-DATA placeholder replaces the actual PEM-encoded cert+key at commit time.
 *  - The certificate is referenced by the gRPC extension-service under system services.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/bootstrap/chassis-port-config.conf  (same bootstrap scope)
 *
 * Variables (example values from spine1_qfx5220-32cd):
 *   $CERT_NAME       e.g. aos_grpc
 *   $SECRET_DATA     e.g. (PEM certificate — replaced by "secret" ## SECRET-DATA)
 */
security {
    certificates {
        local {
            $CERT_NAME {
                "$SECRET_DATA"; ## SECRET-DATA
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/interfaces/external-vlan-tagged.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   Flexible-VLAN-tagged external breakout interface for inter-VRF/WAN exit
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: (none)
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — border leaf external connectivity uses a 400G
 *    port broken into 4×10G sub-ports; only sub-port :0 is wired to the external router.
 *  - flexible-vlan-tagging enables multiple VLAN units on a single physical sub-port,
 *    one unit per VRF (blue=399, red=299 in this JVD).
 *  - native-vlan-id 1 ensures untagged frames are handled correctly.
 *  - Platform interface mapping:
 *    QFX5130-32CD: et-0/0/8:0 (breakout)
 *    QFX5700: et-0/0/8:0 (breakout)
 *    ACX7100-48L: et-0/0/8:0 (breakout)
 *    PTX10001-36MR: et-0/0/8:0 (breakout)
 *    QFX5120-32C (Junos): xe-0/0/0 (native flexible-vlan, no breakout)
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/interfaces/irb-gateway.conf         (L3 units defined on this interface)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf    (VRF references these interface units)
 *  - evo/oam/ipv6-router-advertisement.conf  (RA on external units)
 *  - evo/oam/rstp-bridge.conf                      (rstp-bridge)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $EXT_INTF          e.g. et-0/0/8:0
 *   $PARENT_PORT       e.g. et-0/0/8
 *   $NUM_SUB_PORTS     e.g. 4
 *   $BREAKOUT_SPEED    e.g. 10g
 *   $NATIVE_VLAN       e.g. 1
 */
interfaces {
    $PARENT_PORT {
        description "Breakout $PARENT_PORT";
        number-of-sub-ports $NUM_SUB_PORTS;
        speed $BREAKOUT_SPEED;
    }
    $EXT_INTF {
        description to.mx-external;
        flexible-vlan-tagging;
        native-vlan-id $NATIVE_VLAN;
    }
}
```

## evo/interfaces/fabric-uplink.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   Point-to-point fabric uplink to spine with /31 IPv4 addressing
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: (none)
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — description follows Apstra "facing_<peer>:<port>" convention.
 *  - MTU 9216 at physical layer, 9170 at IP layer (46 bytes reserved for L2 overhead).
 *  - IPv4 /31 point-to-point addressing (RFC 3021) — no broadcast waste.
 *  - Platform interface mapping:
 *    QFX5220-32CD spine: et-0/0/0 through et-0/0/15
 *    QFX5130-32CD border leaf: et-0/0/2, et-0/0/3 (spine-facing)
 *    ACX7100-48L: et-0/0/2, et-0/0/3
 *    PTX10001-36MR: et-0/0/2, et-0/0/3
 *    QFX5700: et-0/0/2, et-0/0/3
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/transport/bgp-underlay-fabric.conf  (BGP sessions run over these links)
 *  - evo/bootstrap/chassis-port-config.conf  (port speed assignment)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $FABRIC_INTF       e.g. et-0/0/2
 *   $PEER_DESCRIPTION  e.g. facing_spine1:et-0/0/5
 *   $LINK_MTU          e.g. 9216
 *   $IP_MTU            e.g. 9170
 *   $LOCAL_IP_SLASH31  e.g. 10.0.1.1/31
 */
interfaces {
    $FABRIC_INTF {
        description $PEER_DESCRIPTION;
        speed 100g;
        mtu $LINK_MTU;
        unit 0 {
            family inet {
                mtu $IP_MTU;
                address $LOCAL_IP_SLASH31;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/interfaces/irb-gateway.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   IRB gateway interface for EVPN/VXLAN symmetric routing (ERB model)
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: (none)
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Border leaf IRB pattern — in the 3-stage ERB design, EVO border leafs use
 *    IRB units on the external VRF breakout interfaces rather than on per-VLAN
 *    fabric segments (those live on the Junos leaf tier).
 *  - The border leaf variant uses the external-vlan-tagged interface as the L3
 *    gateway rather than a vlans/vxlan-mapped IRB; see external-vlan-tagged.conf.
 *  - Dual-stack addressing with per-VRF unit assignment.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/interfaces/external-vlan-tagged.conf  (physical interface carrying these units)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf      (VRF binding)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $EXT_INTF        e.g. et-0/0/8:0
 *   $VLAN_UNIT       e.g. 399
 *   $VLAN_ID         e.g. 399
 *   $UNIT_DESC       e.g. VRF blue to mx-external
 *   $UNIT_IPV4       e.g. 10.200.0.4/31
 *   $UNIT_IPV6       e.g. 2001:db8:dc1:10:200::4/127
 */
interfaces {
    $EXT_INTF {
        unit $VLAN_UNIT {
            description "$UNIT_DESC";
            vlan-id $VLAN_ID;
            family inet {
                address $UNIT_IPV4;
            }
            family inet6 {
                address $UNIT_IPV6;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/interfaces/loopback-multi-unit.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   Multi-unit loopback for router-id, EVPN overlay, and per-VRF identities
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: (none)
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — unit allocation follows Apstra ID assignment.
 *  - Unit 0: router-id + underlay reachability (used as BGP overlay local-address).
 *  - Units 2+: per-VRF loopback for route-distinguisher source (one unit per tenant VRF).
 *  - Spine has only unit 0 (no VRFs); border leaf adds units for each VRF (blue, red).
 *  - Both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses assigned for dual-stack EVPN signaling.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/transport/bgp-evpn-overlay.conf  (overlay BGP uses lo0.0 address)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf (VRF references lo0 unit for RD)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $ROUTER_ID_V4     e.g. 192.168.255.2/32
 *   $ROUTER_ID_V6     e.g. fdf6:ed70:1fac:f2d1::1002/128
 *   $VRF_UNIT         e.g. 2
 *   $VRF_LO_V4        e.g. 192.168.11.0/32
 *   $VRF_LO_V6        e.g. fdf6:ed70:1fac:f2d1::2000/128
 */
interfaces {
    lo0 {
        unit 0 {
            family inet {
                address $ROUTER_ID_V4;
            }
            family inet6 {
                address $ROUTER_ID_V6;
            }
        }
        unit $VRF_UNIT {
            family inet {
                address $VRF_LO_V4;
            }
            family inet6 {
                address $VRF_LO_V6;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/oam/ipv6-router-advertisement.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   IPv6 Router Advertisement on IRB/WAN interfaces
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — RA for external IPv6 interfaces.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/interfaces/external-vlan-tagged.conf  (interfaces listed here)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf      (VRF binds these interfaces)
 *
 * Variables:
 *   $INTERFACE  e.g. et-0/0/8:0.299, et-0/0/8:0.399
 */
protocols {
    router-advertisement {
        interface $INTERFACE {
            max-advertisement-interval 30;
            min-advertisement-interval 10;
        }
        /* ... repeat per IPv6-enabled external interface ... */
    }
}
```

## evo/oam/l2-learning-telemetry.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   L2 learning telemetry for remote MAC entry visibility
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — remote MAC telemetry.
 *
 * Pair with: (standalone — no dependencies)
 */
protocols {
    l2-learning {
        telemetry {
            enable-remote-entries;
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/oam/lldp-discovery.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   LLDP neighbor discovery configuration
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — LLDP for topology discovery.
 *
 * Pair with: (standalone — no dependencies)
 */
protocols {
    lldp {
        port-id-subtype interface-name;
        port-description-type interface-description;
        neighbour-port-info-display port-id;
        interface all;
    }
}
```

## evo/oam/rstp-bridge.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   RSTP bridge configuration with edge-port BPDU blocking
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Spine has `protocols rstp { disable; }` (no L2 on spine).
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/interfaces/external-vlan-tagged.conf  (edge interfaces on EVO border-leaf)
 */
protocols {
    rstp {
        bridge-priority 0;
        bpdu-block-on-edge;
    }
}
```

## evo/oam/sflow-telemetry.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   sFlow telemetry sampling and collector configuration
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — sFlow for traffic telemetry.
 *
 * Pair with: (standalone — no dependencies)
 *
 * Variables:
 *   $SOURCE_IP       e.g. 10.48.9.240
 *   $COLLECTOR_IP    e.g. 10.87.84.51
 *   $COLLECTOR_PORT  e.g. 9995
 *   $INTERFACE       e.g. et-0/0/2.0
 */
protocols {
    sflow {
        polling-interval 20;
        sample-rate {
            ingress 128;
            egress 128;
        }
        source-ip $SOURCE_IP;
        collector $COLLECTOR_IP {
            udp-port $COLLECTOR_PORT;
        }
        interfaces $INTERFACE;
        /* ... repeat per monitored interface ... */
    }
}
```

## evo/policy/bgp-aos-export-policy.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   BGP-AOS-Policy master export filter chaining AllPodNetworks and BGP redistribution
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — master export policy for all fabric BGP sessions.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/policy/pod-network-export.conf  (AllPodNetworks referenced here)
 *  - evo/transport/bgp-underlay-fabric.conf  (fabric BGP applies this)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf          (vrf-evpn-ip-prefix)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $VRF_NAME     e.g. blue
 */
policy-options {
    policy-statement BGP-AOS-Policy {
        term BGP-AOS-Policy-10 {
            from policy AllPodNetworks;
            then accept;
        }
        term BGP-AOS-Policy-20 {
            from protocol bgp;
            then accept;
        }
        term BGP-AOS-Policy-100 {
            then reject;
        }
    }
    policy-statement BGP-AOS-Policy-$VRF_NAME {
        term BGP-AOS-Policy-$VRF_NAME-10 {
            from policy AllPodNetworks-$VRF_NAME;
            then accept;
        }
        term BGP-AOS-Policy-$VRF_NAME-20 {
            from protocol bgp;
            then accept;
        }
        term BGP-AOS-Policy-$VRF_NAME-100 {
            then reject;
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/policy/community-definitions.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   BGP community definitions for fabric signaling and route tagging
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   spine1_qfx5220-32cd spine2_qfx5220-32cd borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — community values are OS-independent.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/policy/leaf-to-spine-evpn-filter.conf   (FROM_SPINE_EVPN_TIER)
 *  - evo/policy/leaf-to-spine-fabric-filter.conf  (FROM_SPINE_FABRIC_TIER)
 *  - evo/policy/external-route-export.conf        (FABRIC_COMMUNITIES delete)
 *  - evo/policy/external-route-import.conf        (RoutesFromExt communities)
 *  - evo/policy/pod-network-export.conf           (DEFAULT_DIRECT, VRF communities)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $VRF_NAME          e.g. blue, red
 *   $VRF_COMM_V4       e.g. BLUE_COMMUNITY_V4
 *   $VRF_MEMBERS_V4    e.g. [ 3:20007 21003:26000 ]
 *   $VRF_COMM_V6       e.g. BLUE_COMMUNITY_V6
 *   $VRF_MEMBERS_V6    e.g. [ 3:20008 21003:26000 ]
 *   $EXT_PEER_NAME     e.g. external_router
 */
policy-options {
    /* Base communities — present on all devices */
    community DEFAULT_DIRECT_V4 members [ 3:20007 21001:26000 ];
    community DEFAULT_DIRECT_V6 members [ 3:20008 21001:26000 ];
    community FROM_SPINE_EVPN_TIER members 0:14;
    community FROM_SPINE_FABRIC_TIER members 0:15;
    community FABRIC_COMMUNITIES members [ 0:12 0:13 0:14 0:15 .+:200.. 2....:260.. ];

    /* Per-VRF communities — one pair (v4 + v6) per VRF on leaf/border-leaf */
    community $VRF_COMM_V4 members $VRF_MEMBERS_V4;
    community $VRF_COMM_V6 members $VRF_MEMBERS_V6;

    /* External-peer import communities — border-leaf only */
    community RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME members [ 3:20009 $VRF_ID:26000 ];
    community RoutesFromExtV6-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME members [ 3:20010 $VRF_ID:26000 ];
}
```

## evo/policy/external-route-export.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   External route export policy with fabric community stripping
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — strips fabric communities before external export.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/policy/route-filter-lists.conf       (RoutesToExt filter-lists)
 *  - evo/policy/community-definitions.conf    (FABRIC_COMMUNITIES definition)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf     (VRF BGP applies this as export)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $VRF_NAME       e.g. blue
 *   $EXT_PEER_NAME  e.g. external_router
 */
policy-options {
    policy-statement RoutesToExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME {
        term RoutesToExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-10 {
            from {
                family inet;
                route-filter-list RoutesToExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME;
            }
            then {
                community delete FABRIC_COMMUNITIES;
                accept;
            }
        }
        term RoutesToExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-20 {
            from {
                family inet6;
                route-filter-list RoutesToExtV6-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME;
            }
            then {
                community delete FABRIC_COMMUNITIES;
                accept;
            }
        }
        term RoutesToExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-30 {
            from family inet;
            then reject;
        }
        term RoutesToExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-40 {
            from family inet6;
            then reject;
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/policy/external-route-import.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   External route import policy with route-filter-list and community tagging
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — import policy for external VRF BGP peers.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/policy/route-filter-lists.conf       (filter-lists referenced here)
 *  - evo/policy/community-definitions.conf    (import communities)
 *  - evo/services/vrf-evpn-ip-prefix.conf     (VRF BGP applies this as import)
 *
 * Variables (example values from borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd):
 *   $VRF_NAME          e.g. blue
 *   $EXT_PEER_NAME     e.g. external_router
 *   $IMPORT_COMMUNITY  e.g. RoutesFromExt-blue-external_router
 *   $IMPORT_COMM_V6    e.g. RoutesFromExtV6-blue-external_router
 */
policy-options {
    policy-statement RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME {
        term RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-10 {
            from {
                family inet;
                route-filter-list RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME;
            }
            then {
                community add $IMPORT_COMMUNITY;
                accept;
            }
        }
        term RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-20 {
            from {
                family inet6;
                route-filter-list RoutesFromExtV6-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME;
            }
            then {
                community add $IMPORT_COMM_V6;
                accept;
            }
        }
        term RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-30 {
            from family inet;
            then reject;
        }
        term RoutesFromExt-$VRF_NAME-$EXT_PEER_NAME-40 {
            from family inet6;
            then reject;
        }
    }
}
```

## evo/policy/leaf-to-spine-evpn-filter.conf

```
/*
 * Topic:   LEAF_TO_SPINE_EVPN_OUT loop-prevention filter rejecting spine-originated EVPN routes
 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
 *   Junos: esi-leaf1_qfx5120-48y esi-leaf2_qfx5120-48y server-leaf1_qfx5120-48y borderleaf1_qfx5120-32c borderleaf2_qfx5120-32c
 *   EVO:   borderleaf1_qfx5130-32cd borderleaf2_qfx5130-32cd
 *
 * Highlights:
 *  - Body is byte-identical to the Junos sibling.
 *  - Generated by Juniper Apstra — loop prevention for eBGP CLOS EVPN overlay.
 *
 * Pair with:
 *  - evo/transport/bgp-evpn-overlay.conf   (applied as export policy)
 *  - evo/policy/community-definitions.conf (FROM_SPINE_EVPN_TIER community)
 */
policy-options {
    policy-statement LEAF_TO_SPINE_EVPN_OUT {
        term LEAF_TO_SPINE_EVPN_OUT-10 {
            from {
                protocol bgp;
                community FROM_SPINE_EVPN_TIER;
            }
            then reject;
        }
        term LEAF_TO_SPINE_EVPN_OUT-20 {
            then accept;
        }
    }
}
```

/*

 * Variant: Evolved-OS (EVO)
 * Seen on:
